reasoning.typography: Generic sans-serif system with no character — could be any B2B SaaS site. The type lacks distinctive personality or discipline beyond standard weights.
reasoning.hierarchy: Hero headline dominates adequately, but below the fold everything flattens into uniform card grids with identical visual weight. Icon-title-description pattern repeats mechanically.
reasoning.distinctiveness: Completely interchangeable. 'Own your AI' + trust logos + three-column features = the exact template used by 1000 enterprise AI companies. Zero visual or structural specificity to Cohere.
reasoning.color: Muted palette with occasional purple accent in imagery, but mostly grayscale. The accent color appears in photography, not as a systematic design element. Safe but unremarkable.
reasoning.density: Generic padding everywhere. Hero has excessive whitespace, then cookie-cutter card grids with uniform spacing. No confidence in density variation or asymmetry.
reasoning.voice: Pure stencil IA: Hero CTA → Trust logos → Three-column value props. The structure could describe any infrastructure product. 'Safe. Flexible. Independent.' could be cloud hosting, databases, anything.

winning_patterns: Large hero headline creates initial impact
Product screenshot provides some tangible proof
anti_patterns: Completely generic B2B SaaS template structure
Trust logo parade is enterprise cliché
No distinctive visual or typographic voice whatsoever
Could swap 'Cohere' for any AI infrastructure company
